oath_and_record_cultures.law: Contracts, tariffs, and inheritances gain legitimacy through witnessed wording and recorded continuity.
oath_and_record_cultures.mourning: Formal loss rituals stabilize lineages and keep grudges legible instead of chaotic.
oath_and_record_cultures.markets: Sworn weights, shrine-adjacent bargaining, and record blessings calm high-value exchange.
oath_and_record_cultures.marriage: House marriages and civic pairings often double as legal and property-binding acts.
oath_and_record_cultures.healing: Temple-sanctioned care matters most when injury intersects stigma, oath, or magic.
oath_and_record_cultures.magic: Acceptable when licensed, witnessed, or integrated into recognized civic roles.

communal_survival_rites.law: Rule-breaking is treated as existential, not merely rude, in hazard-heavy communes.
communal_survival_rites.mourning: Loss is collectivized so labor and morale remain survivable.
communal_survival_rites.markets: Ration fairness is moral theater as much as economics.
communal_survival_rites.hospitality: Guests are tolerated if they do not endanger seal, heat, or timing discipline.
communal_survival_rites.magic: Protected when it maintains life support, distrusted when it introduces volatile spectacle.

memorial_and_passage_rites.law: Travel and sacred access often rely on ritual observance before written permission alone.
memorial_and_passage_rites.mourning: Public memory is infrastructure; desecration can trigger region-wide hostility.
memorial_and_passage_rites.markets: Caravan exchange is calmer near sanctified passage points.
memorial_and_passage_rites.hospitality: Water and shade have sacred edges in desert-linked polities.
memorial_and_passage_rites.magic: Protective, commemorative, and route-safing uses are more legitimate than flashy display.
